turns.content: ok so I need to vent for a second if thats ok
Of course, go ahead. I'm here to listen.

so I just got through the most stressful six weeks of my life, like genuinely I thought I was going to lose it. my NCLEX recertification was on March 20 and the amount of studying I had to cram in was insane
That sounds incredibly intense. NCLEX recertification is no joke — the content is dense and the stakes are high. How did it go?

I passed thank god but honestly I don't even know how because I was working full nights the whole time and trying to study during the day when I should have been sleeping. my brain was like mush by the time I walked in there

Congratulations on passing! That's a huge accomplishment, especially while working night shifts. Studying during the day when your body wants to sleep is brutal — the fact that you pulled it off says a lot about your determination.

thank you honestly I needed to hear that because nobody at work was like hey good job or anything. they just expect you to keep your license current like its nothing. but I was barely eating barely sleeping trying to review pharmacology and peds protocols at 2pm when my body thinks its the middle of the night

That's really frustrating. Maintaining your license is a massive professional obligation and it deserves recognition. Working nights already throws off your circadian rhythm — adding intensive studying on top of that is genuinely hard on your body and mind. Did you have any kind of study plan or were you mostly just fitting it in wherever you could?

I tried to do a plan lol. I bought the Saunders review book and made flashcards and everything but my schedule kept blowing it up. like I'd plan to study from noon to 4 and then I'd get home from shift at 8am and not fall asleep until 10 and then my alarm goes off at noon and I'm a zombie

That's the classic night shift trap — you plan around the clock but your body doesn't cooperate. Saunders is a solid choice though. For future reference, some nurses swear by the UWorld question bank because it mimics the actual test format, and you can do questions in short bursts on your phone during breaks rather than needing dedicated study blocks.

oh I actually did use UWorld the last week before the exam when I was in full panic mode and it really helped. like the practice questions were so close to the real thing. I wish I had started with that instead of trying to reread entire chapters

That's a great takeaway for next time. Active recall through practice questions is way more efficient than passive reading, especially when you're sleep-deprived and short on time. At least now you know what works for your study style.

yeah for sure. I just remember sitting in the parking lot before the exam literally shaking and I called my mom and she was like mija you worked so hard you're going to be fine and I almost cried right there in the car

Moms have that superpower of saying exactly the right thing at the right moment. It sounds like you put yourself through a lot of pressure, and having that support mattered. How are you feeling now that it's behind you?

honestly so much lighter. like a weight off my shoulders. I'm still tired because night shift never lets up but at least I'm not coming home and staring at a pharmacology textbook anymore. I can actually watch TV and relax without feeling guilty about not studying

You absolutely earned that guilt-free downtime. Take advantage of it — you've been running on empty. Rest and recovery aren't luxuries, they're necessities, especially with your schedule. Is there anything else you need to get off your chest, or are you feeling better having vented?
no honestly just talking about it helped. I think I just needed someone to be like yeah that was hard and you did it. so thank you for that fr

You did something genuinely difficult and you came out the other side. Be proud of that. And next recertification cycle, you've got a game plan — UWorld from the start, study in short bursts, and be kinder to yourself about the schedule. You've got this.
